title: Étienne Sinotte
text:
Welcome!
I am a PhD Student in the Department of Political Science at McGill University. I primarily work in comparative politics, with a regional focus on Latin America (and, sometimes, Canada).
My research examines the relationship between displacement, democratic erosion, and social mobilization in Latin America. I ask why states recognize some forms of displacement while rendering others legally invisible, and what this reveals about stratified citizenship and criminal governance patterns in the region. This grows out of earlier empirical work in Peru on collaborative governance, which first drew my attention to the gap between formal institutional frameworks and the communities the state excludes.
I hold an MSc in Political Science from the Université de Montréal, and a BA in Applied Political Studies from the Université de Sherbrooke.

title: Research
text:
Projects
Uprooted Democracy: Displacement and the Politics of Recognition

Examines why states recognize some forms of displacement (climate-, development-, and persecution-led) while rendering others legally invisible, and what this reveals about democratic quality in Latin America



Publications
Sinotte, Étienne. (Under review; working paper available). “Making Collaborative Governance Work in Contexts of State Weakness and Disengagement: Peru’s Indigenous COVID-19 Commands.”

Explains subnational variation the effectiveness of Peru’s indigenous COVID Commands through pre-existing collaborative networks and NGO involvement
Shows how civil society can sustain coordination in medium-capacity states when the state disengages

Brouard, François, Natalya Brown, Manuel Litalien and Étienne Sinotte. 2025. “Definitions of northern/rural/remote/peripheric communities in Canada.” In Research Companion to the Periphery and Peripheral Regions: Concepts, Diagnosis, Dilemmas, edited by Jerzy Banski. Edward Elgar Publishing.

Reviews competing definitions of northern, rural, remote, and peripheric communities in Canada, mapping four key continua (northern/southern, rural/urban, remote/close, peripheric/core)
Argues that relying on any single classification risks oversimplifying the realities of smaller communities, with consequences for welfare programs and philanthropic efforts



Book Reviews
Sinotte, Étienne. 2025. “Climate Displacement By Jamie Draper Oxford, United Kingdom: Oxford University Press, 2023. 272pp. £90.00/ US$115.00. ISBN 978 0192 870162.” Journal of Refugee Studies 38 (3): 813–15.
Sinotte, Étienne. 2024. “Recognition Politics: Indigenous Rights and Ethnic Conflict in the Andes: By Lorenza B. Fontana, Cambridge, Cambridge University Press, 2023, 250 Pp., US$99.99 (Hardcover), ISBN 9781009265515.” Canadian Journal of Latin American and Caribbean Studies / Revue Canadienne Des Études Latino-Américaines et Caraïbes 49 (1): 117–19.
Sinotte, Étienne. 2023. “Voice and Inequality: Poverty and Political Participation in Latin American Democracies, de Carew Boulding et Claudio A. Holzner, New York, Oxford University Press, 2021, 244 p.” Politique et Sociétés 42 (3): 218.


Media
Sinotte, Étienne. 2026. “Ousting of Peru’s president points to a deeper crisis.” The Conversation Canada. March 5, 2026.
